Re:
Farn Polrrrcel PRACrrcEs CoMMrssroN
-128 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacranrenro. CA 95814-2329
(916) -122--s660 o Fax (9 l6) -122-0886
March 5,2008
Ronald R. Stevens
Assistant C ity Attorney
City of Modesto
1010 Tenth Street. Suite 6300
P.O. Box 642
Modesto, California 95353
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-08-004
Dear Mr. Stevens:
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of the Modesto City Council
regarding the campaign reporting provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1
Because your letter seeks general information, we are treating it as a request for informal
asslstance.-
QUESTION
May the Modesto City Council enact an ordinance requiring non-local campaign
committees to file campaign disclosure statements, such as a statement of organization and
independent expenditure reports, with the local entity when the committee spends money to
influence a local election?
CONCLUSION
Nothing in the Act prevents a local agency from imposing additional requirements
on any person if the requirements do not prevent the person from complying with the Act.
However, a local government agency may not enact any ordinances that impose additional
or different filing obligations from those imposed by the Act unless the obligations apply
only to persons active solely in the local jurisdiction.
' Government Code sections 8 1000-9 1014. Commission regulations appear at title 2. sections l8 109-
Iti997. of the Calitbrnia Code of Regularions.
r Infbrmal assistance cloes not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
fbrmal written advice. (Section tl3l l4; regulation 18329(cX3). copy enclosed).


File No. I-08-004
Page No. 2
FACTS
A southern California based political committee sent a campaign mailer negative to
one of the candidates ntnning for Stanislaus County Supervisor whbse opponent was a
Modesto City Councilmember. The committee, the California Republican Assembly, filed
reports with the Secretary of State but did not file an independent expenditure report with the
Stanislaus County Clerk.
ANALYSIS
Section 81013 provides:
"Nothing in this title prevents the Legislature or any other state or
local agency from imposing additional requirements on any person if
the requirements do not prevent the person from complying with this
title.. . ."
However, Section 81009.5(b) restricts local ordinances from imposing additional
filing requirements on certain groups.
"Notwithstanding Section 82013, no local government agency shall
enact any ordinance imposing filing requirements additional to or
different from those set forth in chapter 4 (commencing with Section
84100) for elections held in its jurisdiction unless the additional of
different filing requirements apply only to the candidates seeking
election in that jurisdiction, their controlled committees formed or
existing primarily to support or oppose their candidacies, and to
committees formed or existing primarily to support or oppose their
candidacies, and to committees formed or existing primaiily to support
or oppose a candidate or to support or oppose the qualification of, or
passage of, a local ballot measure [that] is being voted on only in the
jurisdiction, and to city or county general purpose committees active
only in that city or county, respectively,"
Accordingly, the Modesto City Council may not enact any ordinance that imposes
additional or different filing requirements on non-local committees or that prevents those
committees from complying with the Act. Because the California Republican Assembly is a
statewide committee it, and any other committees not operating excluiively within the city,s
jurisdiction, are not subject to any local ordinances regarding filing requirements, including
filing requirements for independent expenditures, that are diifereni fro1n those imposed by
the Act.
With respect to the filing requirements imposed by the Act, a committee making
independent expenditures is required to comply with Section 84203.5(a), which states:
"(a) In addition to any campaign statements required by this
article, if a candidate or committee has made independent


File No. I-08-004
Page No. 3
expenditures totaling one thousand dollars ($ 1,000) or more in a
calendar year to support or oppose a candidate, a measure or
qualification of a measllre, it shatl file inclepenclent expenditure
reports at the sante time, covering the same periods, crnd in the
places where the candidctte or committee woulcl be required to fite
campaign statements under this article, as if it were.firmed or'
existing primarily to sttpport or oppose the candidate or measure
or qualification of the tneasure.,, (Emphasis aelded.)
Section 84215 identifies where campaign reports are to be filed. For candidates for
local county elections, subdivision (d) providei:
"(d) County elected officers, municipal court judges, candidates for
these offices, their controlled committee, commitiees formed or
existing primarily to sttpport or oppose candidates or local measures to
be voted upon in any number ofjurisdictions within one county, other
than those specified in subdivision (e), and county general purpose
committees shall file the original and one copy with the clerk of tn"
county."
Accordingly, under the provisions of the Act as applied to the facts you have
submitted, the California Republican Assembly is requirid to fit" a supplemental
independent expenditure report, for the period covered, with the Staniilaus County Clerk if
the independent expenditure was g1,000 o, more.3 Therefore, if the Modesto City Council
were to adopt an ordinance requiring a non-local committee to file an independent
expenditure report with the city, it would be dupl,icative of the requirements already provided
by the Act, assuming the same $1,000 threshold.a
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Counsel
By: William
Senior Counsel, Legal Division
